title: panicum larcomianum
intro: Panicum larcomianum is a species of grass native to Australia, known for its medicinal properties and traditional uses by indigenous Australians. The plant is traditionally used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Medicinally, Panicum larcomianum has been used to treat fever and rheumatic complaints, with the leaves and roots being used to make herbal preparations. The plant's leaves and roots can be made into teas, infusions, or poultices, and have been traditionally used to promote wound healing and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
